entries.name: CancelTaskRequest
entries.reason: The SDK strips the JSON-RPC envelope from typed messages: jsonrpc/id live on JSONRPCRequest/JSONRPCNotification/JSONRPCResponse/JSONRPCError in mcp.types.jsonrpc and are attached by the session layer. The 2025-11-25 schema restructure merged the envelope into every typed request/notification def.

entries.name: CancelTaskResult
entries.reason: ext-tasks schema.json lost the Task half of the CancelTaskResult intersection (Result & Task) at the ts-to-zod boundary: the def has only the optional _meta property and no required array, so field presence and requiredness are unverifiable. (CreateTaskResult and GetTaskResult survived with their required arrays intact, so only this def is exempted.)

entries.name: CancelTaskResult
entries.reason: ext-tasks schema.json lost the Task half of the CancelTaskResult intersection (Result & Task) at the ts-to-zod boundary, so the def carries only _meta; the SDK models the 2025-11-25 core def, which requires the task fields. See the REQUIRED-UNVERIFIABLE exemption for this def.

entries.name: CreateTaskResult
entries.reason: The SDK restores the 2025-11-25 core schema's tasks types; the experimental tasks-extension schema revises these defs (flattened result fields, ttl renamed to ttlMs, added pollIntervalMs/statusMessage). The extension itself is not implemented in the SDK.

entries.name: DetailedTask
entries.reason: ext-tasks schema.json lost every core $ref at the ts-to-zod import boundary (vacuous anyOf:[{},{},{}] collapsed to Any by the generator); followup-1 section 2.3, re-verified at pin dd47977f. The intended type is the InputRequest/InputResponse union of core draft types.

entries.reason: Named spec constant, not a schema $defs entry; the generated oracles model defs only.

entries.name: CLIENT_INFO_META_KEY

entries.name: CancelTaskRequestParams
entries.reason: Inline params object lifted to a model; the schema defines these params inline on the request def.

entries.name: CancelTaskResult
entries.reason: resultType (added in 2026-07-28; absent means complete) is defined once on the SDK's shared Result base, so every result class carries the field; this def's last schema revision predates 2026-07-28, so no oracle version of the paired def has it.

entries.name: CreateMessageResult
entries.reason: resultType (added in 2026-07-28; absent means complete) is defined once on the SDK's shared Result base, so every result class carries the field; this def's last schema revision predates 2026-07-28, so no oracle version of the paired def has it.

entries.name: CreateMessageResultWithTools
entries.reason: SDK splits the spec's CreateMessageResult into a backwards-compatible single-content result and a with-tools variant supporting array content; the spec has one def.

entries.name: CreateTaskResult
entries.reason: resultType (added in 2026-07-28; absent means complete) is defined once on the SDK's shared Result base, so every result class carries the field; this def's last schema revision predates 2026-07-28, so no oracle version of the paired def has it.

entries.name: ElicitRequestedSchema
entries.reason: The SDK keeps elicitation requestedSchema as an untyped dict (ElicitRequestedSchema = dict[str, Any]) instead of modeling the restricted JSON Schema subset; typing it is a pending curation decision for the superset design.

entries.name: ElicitResult
entries.reason: resultType (added in 2026-07-28; absent means complete) is defined once on the SDK's shared Result base, so every result class carries the field; this def's last schema revision predates 2026-07-28, so no oracle version of the paired def has it.

entries.name: ElicitationRequiredErrorData
entries.reason: SDK-curated shape for the URLElicitationRequiredError error.data payload; the 2025-11-25 schema models it inline on the named error (which the SDK has not implemented as a type - see URLElicitationRequiredError entry).

entries.name: MissingRequiredClientCapabilityErrorData
entries.reason: SDK-curated shape for a named JSON-RPC error's data payload (error codes added in 2026-07-28); the schema models the data inline on the named error def, which the SDK represents as an error-code constant plus this data model.

entries.name: PROTOCOL_VERSION_META_KEY
entries.reason: Named spec constant, not a schema $defs entry; the generated oracles model defs only.

entries.name: SamplingContent
entries.reason: SDK-curated narrowing union (text/image/audio) used by the backwards-compatible CreateMessageResult; the spec models sampling result content inline.

entries.name: UNSUPPORTED_PROTOCOL_VERSION
entries.reason: Named spec constant, not a schema $defs entry; the generated oracles model defs only.

entries.name: UnsupportedProtocolVersionErrorData

entries.name: Cursor
entries.reason: The SDK inlines cursors as plain str fields (cursor/nextCursor) instead of exporting the Cursor alias.

entries.name: Notification
entries.reason: Generic base class: Request/Notification take params as a TypeVar field with no default, so pydantic reports it required. Every concrete request/notification type overrides params with the spec's per-method requiredness.

entries.name: Request
entries.reason: Generic base class: Request/Notification take params as a TypeVar field with no default, so pydantic reports it required. Every concrete request/notification type overrides params with the spec's per-method requiredness.

entries.name: Root
entries.reason: SDK uses FileUrl where the schema says format:uri; the spec description for Root.uri states it MUST start with file:// for now, so the SDK encodes the prose constraint in the type.

entries.name: Cursor
entries.reason: The SDK inlines cursors as plain str fields (cursor/nextCursor) instead of exporting the Cursor alias.

entries.name: JSONRPCBatchRequest
entries.reason: JSON-RPC batching existed only in the 2025-03-26 schema (removed in 2025-06-18); the SDK never implemented it and the spec dropped it.

entries.name: Icons
entries.reason: Icons is an interface mixin ({icons: Icon[]}); the SDK declares the icons field directly on each type that has one instead of exporting the mixin.

entries.name: JSONRPCErrorResponse
entries.reason: SDK requires the id key on error responses; the 2025-11-25+ schemas list only error/jsonrpc as required. JSON-RPC 2.0 requires id on every response (null when no request id could be determined) and every earlier schema version agrees, so the optional id looks like a spec-side regression; the SDK keeps the required-but-nullable id until the spec settles it.

entries.name: JSONArray
entries.reason: 2026-07-28 JSON aliases; the SDK uses builtins (Any / dict[str, Any] / list[Any]) instead of named aliases (see _spec_names.SCHEMA_NOT_MODELED).

entries.name: JSONObject
entries.reason: 2026-07-28 JSON aliases; the SDK uses builtins (Any / dict[str, Any] / list[Any]) instead of named aliases (see _spec_names.SCHEMA_NOT_MODELED).

entries.name: JSONRPCErrorResponse
entries.reason: SDK requires the id key on error responses; the 2025-11-25+ schemas list only error/jsonrpc as required. JSON-RPC 2.0 requires id on every response (null when no request id could be determined) and every earlier schema version agrees, so the optional id looks like a spec-side regression; the SDK keeps the required-but-nullable id until the spec settles it.

entries.reason: The SDK strips the JSON-RPC envelope from typed messages: jsonrpc/id live on JSONRPCRequest/JSONRPCNotification/JSONRPCResponse/JSONRPCError in mcp.types.jsonrpc and are attached by the session layer. The 2025-11-25 schema restructure merged the envelope into every typed request/notification def.

entries.name: Root
entries.reason: SDK uses FileUrl where the schema says format:uri; the spec description for Root.uri states it MUST start with file:// for now, so the SDK encodes the prose constraint in the type.

entries.name: SingleSelectEnumSchema
entries.reason: The SDK keeps elicitation requestedSchema as an untyped dict (ElicitRequestedSchema = dict[str, Any]) instead of modeling the restricted JSON Schema subset; typing it is a pending curation decision for the superset design.

entries.name: UnsupportedProtocolVersionError
entries.reason: Named-error wrapper interface; the generic ErrorData envelope plus the error-code constants (and the modeled *ErrorData payloads) cover every wire value (see _spec_names.SCHEMA_NOT_MODELED).
